criteria_results.criterion: Triggering an achievement drop shows a card with the new chrome (screws, tier banner, portrait image, stats footer).
criteria_results.evidence: User screenshot of revealed Drumbeano Crocodilio card: chrome present (banner #002, FRG·02 stamp, name plate, flavor, stats, footer) but portrait area shows only the dark gradient — no voxel image, no emoji fallback. Playwright smoke had reported 'all 31 portrait assets loaded, no portrait 404s', which was a status-200 check, not a pixel-painted check.

process_issues.description: Playwright check verified HTTP 200 on portrait WebPs but did not assert that the images were actually painted at non-zero rect. The smoke checklist treated 'no 404s' as equivalent to 'visible art', which it is not. The same false-positive likely hid blank portraits in the Drops grid too.

failure_output: User-reported regression after PR #5 merged: 'revealed cards dont have the actual character on them?' Live DOM probe via Claude Preview Chromium confirmed the portrait-img element mounts and the WebP fetches 200, but in the reveal-popup pathway the image never reaches paintable state. Direct render bypassing the reveal animation (drops grid) shows the same risk. Root cause hypothesis: `loading="lazy"` on the img + `content-visibility: auto` on the `.cell` wrapper interact badly when the cell is rendered inside an `opacity: 0` ancestor (`#drumrotReveal`); content-visibility's layout skip plus lazy's intersection-observer trigger conspire so the image element never enters a paintable state.
suggested_follow_up: Smoke ladder rung must include a *pixel* assertion: `getBoundingClientRect().width > 0 && img.complete && img.naturalWidth > 0` for the portrait-img after the reveal animation completes. Add to docs/testing/manual-smoke.md and to any Playwright spec that exercises the Drops/reveal flow. Update smoke-test-writer to call this out as the default for any 'images load' criterion.
